title: Create a Superhero Profile App with URL mapping

source.module: Core Django fundamentals - Views and URLs

topicTags: Core Django fundamentals - Views and URLs

content.problemStatement:
In this practice problem, you'll create a Superhero Profile App using Django’s URL mapping with include().

### Task
Your task is to complete the URL configuration to properly route requests to the appropriate views for the home page and the superhero profile pages.
- Update the import path and include function in `heroes/urls.py` in the main project
- Update the URL patterns for 3 superhero profile views in the `superheroes/urls.py` in the app. The URLs should be of the forms:
   - http://some-url.com/superheroes/ (Welcome to the Superheroes Database)
   - http://some-url.com/superheroes/spiderman/ (Spider-Man’s profile)
   - http://some-url.com/superheroes/ironman/ (Iron Man’s profile)
   - http://some-url.com/superheroes/wonderwoman/ (Wonder Woman's profile)

### Setting up the project: 
The project, app and view has already been setup for you. \
**Project name:** `heroes` \
**App name:** `superheroes` \
**View:** The view has already been designed. Check it out on the File Explorer
